name: Barranco Ancho Loop
name_original: Sierra de San Just – circular desde Castel de Cabras
description: A difficult 30.5-mile touring cycling route in Sierra de San Just, offering rugged mountain scenery and 3680 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Castel De Cabra traverse a diverse natural landscape within the Iberian System, characterized by river forests, mountainous massifs, and deep ravines. The village itself is situated at an altitude of 1,088 meters in the Sierra de San Just, alongside the Cabra River. This region offers varied terrain, including significant fluvial landscapes and challenging climbs, making it suitable for touring cyclists seeking natural exploration. The area's position provides varied settings for cycling, with potential for elevation changes and panoramic views. Best touring cycling routes around Castel De Cabra The most popular touring cycling route is Barranco Ancho Loop, a 30.5 miles (49.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route features challenging climbs and offers panoramic views of the surrounding mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Aldehuela Gorge – Puerto de Majalinos loop from Gargallo, a difficult 59.5 miles (95.8 km) path. This route leads through gorges and mountainous landscapes, providing extensive elevation changes. Local touring cyclists also love the Estercuel – Obón loop from Estercuel, a 25.2 miles (40.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain and scenic views, often completed in about 3 hours 31 minutes. Touring cycling around Castel De Cabra is defined by mountainous terrain, riverine forests, and deep ravines. The network offers options for different ability levels, though most routes are challenging. The routes in Castel De Cabra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Castel De Cabra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Castel De Cabra?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes available around Castel De Cabra, offering diverse terrain for exploration.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Castel De Cabra?
A: The touring cycling routes in Castel De Cabra are generally challenging, characterized by mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes. All routes listed in the guide are rated as difficult, making them suitable for experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Castel De Cabra?
A: You can expect to traverse a diverse natural landscape, including river forests, mountainous massifs, and deep ravines. The region is particularly known for its fluvial landscapes, such as the Cabra River, and the protected Martín River Gorges, which offer scenic views and opportunities for birdwatching.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in Castel De Cabra?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31456404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldehuela Gorge – Puerto de Majalinos loop from Canizar del Olivar&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 78.7 miles (126.7 km), offering extensive elevation changes and a full day of cycling.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Castel De Cabra are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957104700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco Ancho Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 30.5-mile (49.1 km) circular trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848923" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estercuel – Obón loop from Estercuel&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent circular option.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Castel De Cabra?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the challenging climbs, panoramic views, and the natural beauty of the mountainous terrain and riverine forests.

Q: Are there any notable natural attractions or landmarks to see along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, while cycling, you might encounter several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3793681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in Barranco de la Hoz Mala on the Guadalope River&lt;/a&gt; is a beautiful natural feature. You could also pass through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1637345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Majalinos&lt;/a&gt;, a mountain pass offering great views.

Q: Can I combine my cycling trip with cultural exploration in the area?
A: Absolutely. The region has cultural heritage, such as the 16th-century Church of the Assumption in Castel de Cabra. Additionally, you might pass through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estercuel&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6860084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cañadilla&lt;/a&gt;, which offer glimpses into local life.

Q: Is there a route that explores the gorges in the region?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31847100" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldehuela Gorge – Puerto de Majalinos loop from Gargallo&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that leads through impressive gorges and mountainous landscapes, showcasing the region's dramatic fluvial features.

Q: Are there any routes known for their quiet roads and natural exploration?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31847972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Silent Route: The Glove loop from Ejulve&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option for those seeking quiet roads and natural exploration, offering a serene cycling experience through varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Castel De Cabra?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for elevation changes, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in Castel De Cabra, with milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, and winters may present colder, more challenging conditions.
tips: Perfect track for gravel. Very fast. Spectacular landscape with shade of pine trees. Possibly the best track in the area.
Beautiful views from the Mirador de Sant Just and with an entertaining but at the same time technical descent through a section of loose stones if we go to the town of Escucha.

At this point we find the beautiful and solitary Hermitage of Sant Just, belonging to the municipality of Cuevas de Almudén. By bicycle you can access it in just over 30 minutes from Cuevas or also through the Sierra de Sant Just. In the portico we can see the date it was built; 1758.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Sierra de San Just – circular from Castel de Cabras route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is classified as difficult. Given the rugged mountain terrain of the Sierra de San Just, cyclists should expect significant elevation changes and potentially challenging sections. It requires good to very good fitness and may include varied surfaces.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this touring bicycle route is approximately 4 hours and 25 minutes (15,930 seconds). This is an average, and your actual time may vary based on fitness, stops, and trail conditions.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de San Just is a rugged mountain range, so expect a mix of surfaces. While some sections may be paved, it's highly probable that you'll encounter unpaved roads and gravel sections, similar to other mountainous regions in Spain. A touring bike suitable for varied terrain, including potentially rougher tracks, is recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You can experience the natural beauty of the Barranco de la Umbría del Buitre, enjoy panoramic views from the Mirador de Sant Just, and see the historic Ermita de Sant Just.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route from Castel de Cabras?
faqs.answer: Specific designated parking for this exact route in Castel de Cabras is not detailed. However, as a starting point for a local route, you would typically find street parking within the village. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for any restrictions.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach Castel de Cabras?
faqs.answer: Information regarding direct public transport links to Castel de Cabras for accessing this specific cycling route is limited. In rural mountainous regions like the Sierra de San Just, public transport options can be infrequent or non-existent. Driving to the starting point is generally the most reliable option.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route in the Sierra de San Just. The trails and roads are generally open for public access.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de San Just is known for being one of the colder and more rugged regions in Aragon. Therefore, the best time to cycle would likely be during the late spring, summer, and early autumn months when temperatures are milder and snow is less likely at higher elevations. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' classification and the rugged, mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes, this route is generally not recommended for beginners or young families. It requires a good level of fitness and experience with touring cycling on varied surfaces.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de San Just – circular from Castel de Cabras route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on this route, it's important to consider the difficulty and length of the trail. If bringing a dog, ensure they are fit enough for a challenging, long ride and that you adhere to local leash laws and clean-up regulations, especially near villages or protected areas.

faqs.question: What should I consider regarding my bike choice for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the likely mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, including potential gravel sections, a touring bicycle suitable for varied terrain is highly recommended. Ensure your bike is equipped with appropriate tires for stability and comfort on rougher tracks.
